subject: Fisheries (Alternative A)

preamble.description: Fisheries is important to the economic development of West Africa and this syllabus has been structured to guide the assessment of learners' knowledge and entrepreneurial skills in fisheries and related vocations. It is also to guide the assessment in practically oriented knowledge and skills in fisheries.

aims_and_objectives: The importance of fisheries in the socio-economic development of West Africa
The dangers of over fishing practices
The regulations governing fishing practices in the country
The differences between freshwater, brackish water and marine habitats and resources
Basic entrepreneurship skills in fisheries related vocations and business
The effects of water pollution on fishery resources
requirements: Schools offering fisheries must have at least an aquarium and a fish pond/concrete tank.
The study of fisheries should be supplemented by visits to well established fish farms, fisheries research institutions, fishing companies and other institutions related to fisheries.

Candidates should keep practical notebooks which should contain records of activities based on laboratory and individual observations carried out in aquaria and fish farms, field trips and also records of specimens collected.
Schools should prepare an album of fishery organisms, fishing gear and craft and different fish rearing facilities and equipment for teaching purposes.

papers.description: Will consist of fifty multiple choice objective questions, all of which must be answered within 1 hour for 50 marks.
Will consist of six essay-type questions. Candidates will be required to answer four questions within 2 hours for 20 marks each.

Will be a practical paper for school candidates or alternative to practical work test for private candidates. It will consist of three questions all of which must be answered within 2 hours for 60 marks.

subtopics.title: (a) Meaning of fisheries
notes: Explanation of the term fisheries.

subtopics.title: (b) Types of fisheries
notes: Culture fisheries (aquaculture)
Capture fisheries (fishing)
Subsistence fisheries
Artisanal fisheries
Commercial fisheries
Industrial fisheries

subtopics.title: (c) Importance of fisheries to national development
notes: Role of fisheries in the national economy e.g. food, employment, income generation, social and cultural life.

subtopics.title: (a) Identification and description of common fishery organisms
notes: Fin fishes (e.g. herring, tuna, tilapia, Clarias, Heterobranchus)
Crustaceans (shrimp/prawns/lobster, crabs)
Molluscs (clam, scallops, oyster, cuttle fish/squid)

subtopics.title: (b) Fishery habitats
notes: Characteristics of habitats: freshwater (river, lake), brackish water (estuary, lagoon) and marine (pelagic, demersal).

subtopics.title: (c) Identification and description of the characteristics of invasive alien species in fishery habitats
notes: Species such as Eichhornia crassipes (water hyacinth), Cyperus papyrus (Papyrus reed), Salvinia molesta (kariba weed), Limnocharis flava (Limnocharis), Pistia stratiotes (water lettuce), Azolla filiculoides (water fern), Enteromorpha flexura (filamentous algae), Ceratophyllum sp. (Hornwort).

subtopics.title: (d) Effects of invasive alien species in fisheries
notes: Analysis of the effects of aquatic invasive alien species on fishery habitats, fishery organisms and fishers.

subtopics.title: (e) Prevention and control of invasive alien species in fishery habitats
notes: Preventive measures such as awareness creation, screening at entry points and enforcement of plant protection and regulatory laws and control measures both physical and biological.

Grouping of fishery organisms under freshwater, brackish water and marine habitats: Tilapia, Clarias/Heterobranchus, Chrysichthys, Heterotis, Lates, Bagrus, Alestes, Synodontis, Prawns, Crabs, Grey mullet, Shrimps, Sardinella, Sea bream, Cassava fish, Tuna, Mackerel, Anchovy, Shark, Cuttle fish/squid, Clam, Ray, Sea urchin.

subtopics.title: (a) Types of fish landing sites
notes: Identification and location of landing sites: beaches, harbours, lagoons, river banks, lake shores.

subtopics.title: (b) Facilities and activities at fish landing sites
notes: Use of facilities: winch, cold store, ice plant, fuel station, slipway, dry dock, jetty and breakwater.
Description of activities: unloading, fuelling, loading ice, beaching for repairs, maintenance, fish processing, fish marketing.

subtopics.title: (c) Sanitation practices at fish landing sites
notes: Proper disposal of wastes generated including oil spills and vessel parts.

subtopics.title: (a) Classification and description of fishing gear
notes: Active fishing gear: cast net, seine net, trawl, dredges, scoop net.
Passive fishing gear: hooking devices, stationary nets, tangle nets, traps.
Merits and demerits of using various gear.

subtopics.title: (b) Construction and maintenance of fishing gear
notes: Materials for construction, repair, and basic maintenance of fishing gear.

subtopics.title: (c) Description and maintenance of fishing craft
notes: Canoes, trawlers, purse seiners and accessories (oars, sails, outboard/inboard engines, winches, sonar, radar).

subtopics.title: (d) Fishing methods
notes: Active and passive fishing methods used in inland, coastal and deep sea fishing.

subtopics.title: (e) Harmful fishing practices
notes: Description, effects, prevention, and minimization of harmful fishing practices.

topics.topic: 1. Identification and classification of fishery organisms

subtopics.title: (a) Identification of common fishery organisms by species
notes: Common and scientific names required.

subtopics.title: (b) Classification of common fishery organisms
notes: Classification under phylum and class for Mollusca, Arthropoda, and Echinodermata. Phylum Chordata classified to subclass level.

topics.topic: 2. Structure and function of fishery organisms

subtopics.title: (a) Fish body measurements
notes: Ability to measure total, standard, and fork lengths, and weights.

subtopics.title: (b) External structures and features of fishery organisms
notes: Assessment of molluscs (cuttle fish), crustaceans (shrimp/prawn, crab), cartilaginous fish (shark, ray), and bony fishes (tilapia, Clarias).

subtopics.title: (c) Internal organs of bony fishes and their functions
notes: Gills, alimentary canal, heart and blood vessels, kidneys, and gonads.

subtopics.title: (a) Locomotion
notes: Role of muscles and fins in movement and balance (pitching, rolling, yawing).

subtopics.title: (b) Feeding and digestion
notes: Ingestion, digestion, absorption, and egestion.

subtopics.title: (c) Blood circulation
notes: Composition, circulation, and functions of blood.

subtopics.title: (d) Gaseous exchange
notes: Mechanism of gaseous exchange.

subtopics.title: (e) Excretion
notes: Osmo-regulation, excretory process, and products.

subtopics.title: (f) Reproduction
notes: Gamete formation, spawning, fertilization, parental care, identification of male/female tilapia, examination of eggs.

subtopics.title: (g) Growth
notes: Life cycle and factors affecting growth (temperature, dissolved oxygen, nutrients, food availability, competition).

subtopics.title: (a) Environmental conditions in fish habitats
notes: Temperature, dissolved oxygen, salinity, pH, turbidity, light, nutrients, upwelling phenomenon; measurement using test kits.

subtopics.title: (b) Ecological processes within fish habitats
notes: Feeding behaviour, predation, competition, food chain, food web, food pyramid, mortality, adaptation.

subtopics.title: (c) Pollution in water bodies
notes: Causes, effects, prevention, and control of pollution on fish populations.

subtopics.title: (a) Principles of Genetics
notes: Chromosomes, genes, genetic crossings, genotype, phenotype; application to fish breeding (super male tilapia, GIFT).

subtopics.title: (b) Inheritance of genetic characteristics
notes: Explanation of inheritance of external characters e.g. skin colour.

subtopics.title: (a) Meaning and importance of aquaculture
notes: Culture of organisms including fish, clams, shrimps, sea weeds.

subtopics.title: (b) Types of aquaculture
notes: State of aquaculture in the country: farm numbers/sizes, cultured species, practices, infrastructure, production levels, prospects, challenges.

subtopics.title: (c) Problems and solutions in aquaculture
notes: Few specialists, high cost of pond construction/feed, difficulty obtaining fingerlings/credit/land, and their solutions.

subtopics.title: (a) Construction of an aquarium
notes: Materials identification, design, and construction.

subtopics.title: (b) Management of an aquarium
notes: Suitable species identification, capture, transport, stocking, and record keeping.

subtopics.title: (a) Introduction to fish farming
notes: Importance, levels (extensive, semi-intensive, intensive), types (monoculture, polyculture, integrated culture), facilities (earthen ponds, cages, concrete tanks, raceways, fish pens).

subtopics.title: (b) Construction of fish culture facilities
notes: Site selection criteria (topography, soil type, water quality/quantity, security), site clearing, marking, excavation, wall formation, drainage fitting, grassing.

subtopics.title: (c) Management of fish ponds
notes: Stocking: species selection criteria, packaging, transport.
Pond maintenance: water level control, leakages, predator/weed control, fertilization.
Water quality control: pH, dissolved oxygen, turbidity, ammonia, temperature, and improvement measures.
Fish feeds and feeding: types, nutrient content, formulation, feeding times/quantities.
Harvesting: partial and total harvesting, gear/methods, draining and refilling.

subtopics.title: (d) Fish diseases
notes: Types/causes (Gill rot fungus, Furunculosis bacteria, Ich protozoa), symptoms, prevention, control, treatment.
Nutrients in fishery organisms (proteins, lipids, mineral salts, water, vitamins) and experiments to test for protein and lipids.

subtopics.title: (a) Meaning of fish processing and preservation
notes: Distinction between preparation for consumption/marketing and extending shelf life.

subtopics.title: (b) Importance of fish processing and preservation
notes: Prevention of spoilage, increasing shelf life, improving taste, adding value.

subtopics.title: (c) General principles
notes: Removal of microbes/water, slowing/denaturing enzymes, slowing bacterial activity, preventing fat oxidation.

subtopics.title: (d) Methods of processing
notes: Washing, scaling, gutting, filleting, and equipment (knives, scissors, mechanical equipment).

subtopics.title: (e) Methods of preservation
notes: Traditional (smoking, cooking, salting, drying, frying), modern (freezing, canning, irradiation, pickling), and equipment (Chorkor smoker).

subtopics.title: (f) Packaging
notes: Materials (cartons, crates, baskets) and demonstration of packaging methods for fresh and preserved fish.

subtopics.title: (g) Products and by-products
notes: Fillets, chunks, canned, smoked, dried, salted, oils, entrails, bones, and their uses.

subtopics.title: (h) Fish spoilage
notes: Signs (sunken eyes, skin mucus, dark gills), causes (microbial, enzymatic, oxidation), effects, public health hazards.

detailed_syllabus.section: F. Fisheries Management and Business of Fisheries

subtopics.title: (a) Meaning and objectives
notes: Maintaining fish stock levels for sustainable exploitation, Maximum Sustainable Yield (MSY), maximizing sustainable catches, maintaining spawning stock.

subtopics.title: (b) Strategies and traditional practices
notes: Limiting fishing units, closures, mesh sizes, quotas, close seasons, taboos, cultural festivals.

subtopics.title: (c) Data collection and analysis
notes: Fish catch, effort, length, weight, age, gear type analysis, and seasonal variations / upwelling.

subtopics.title: (a) Government policies and regulations
notes: Subsidies, stakeholder roles, import/export regulations, juvenile protection.

subtopics.title: (b) International law and conventions
notes: Exclusive Economic Zone (EEZ), IUCN Red List, Convention on Biodiversity (CBD), ICCAT.
Budget preparation, expenditure/income items, cashflow projections, pricing in relation to demand and supply.
Quality control, major centres (Tema, Takoradi, Elmina, Yeji, Mankessim), supply/value chains, food safety standards.

detailed_syllabus.section: G. Practices in Fishing Communities and Fisheries Institutions

notes: Freshwater communities (Yeji, Dambai, Kwamikrom, Abotoase), marine communities (Teshie, Elmina, Chorkor, Shama), festivals (Bakatue, Fetu, Dzawuwu).

topics.topic: 2. Fisheries institutional framework and job opportunities
notes: Water Research Institute, University of Ghana, job opportunities, business creation, extension services.

topics.topic: 1. Fishing gear: Identification, uses and maintenance
2. Fish Identification: Identification and classification of common freshwater, brackish water and marine fishes
3. Identification and description of characteristics of invasive alien species in fishery habitats
4. Fish structure and function (external features, internal structures related to function)
5. Environmental conditions in fish habitats (temperature, dissolved oxygen, pH, salinity)
6. Ecological processes within the aquatic environment (food chain, web, pyramid)
7. Characteristic features of fresh and spoiled fish
8. Identification of micro-organisms and macro-organisms in spoiled fish
9. Fish processing and preservation (processed forms, preserved forms, equipment)
12. Feed formulation and feeding (ingredients, types, methods)
13. Pond fertilization (types, uses, methods of application)
14. Fish diseases (gill rot, furunculosis, ich by symptoms)
